content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

August 13, 1997

Robert Bills
Walton Fire Protection District
211 South Walton Avenue
Yuba City, California 95993

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-386

Dear Mr. Bills:

This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please bear in mind that nothing in this letter should be
construed as evaluation of any conduct which may already have taken place. Further, this letter
is based on the facts as they have been presented to us. The Commission does not act as the
finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

Do you have a conflict of interest that would prevent your participation in any vote of the
board of directors of the Walton Fire Protection District concerning issues that affect possible
annexation or revenue agreements with Yuba City?

CONCLUSION

You have no conflict of interest under the Act and, therefore, may participate in the
decisions of the board of directors of the Walton Fire Protection District to consider possible
annexation or revenue agreements with Yuba City.

FACTS

The Walton Fire Protection District (the "District") is a Special District formed more than
35 years ago in Sutter County to provide fire protection and emergency medical service. The
District serves 20 square miles of urban and rural area in Sutter County, with a population of

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-97-386
Page 2

more than 21,000. Significantly, the District has as its eastern border, the Yuba City limits.
Yuba City operates its own fire department. There are also county fire departments bordering the
District to the west, north and south.

The District is governed by a five member elected board of directors. Board members
serve four year terms and are elected at large from the District. The approximate $685,000
budget of the District is derived nearly entirely from property tax revenues.

The biggest issue facing the District is the expansion of Yuba City and the annexation of
the District's service area, and concomitant with that, an erosion of the District's revenue source
via property tax dollars. The District has proposed to continue to serve areas that are annexed
into the city but which remain closer to existing District stations under a policy known as
"annexation without detachment" or under a "drop boundary agreement." However, the city has
opposed this.

You are employed as a full-time firefighter for the Yuba City Fire Department. You are
also one of the elected members of the District's board of directors.² You have no "decision-
making" authority within the Yuba City Fire Department, but you do have decision-making
authority in your capacity as one of the directors of the District's board of directors. The board
of directors anticipates deliberating and voting on issues that affect annexations into Yuba City
and out of the District's service area, property tax exchange agreements and the like. These
decisions by the board of directors will have a financial impact on both the District and Yuba
City.

ANALYSIS

The District is a "local government agency" as that term is defined in Section 82041
("'local government agency' means a county, city or district of any kind .....").³ As an elected
director to the District's board of directors, you are a public official of a local government agency
under Section 82048 ("'public official' means every member, officer, employee or consultant of
a state or local government agency ...."). Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in, or using their official position to influence a governmental decision in which
they know or have reason to know they have a financial interest.

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it

² You may wish to inquire with both the District and the Yuba City Fire Department regarding whether
your concurrent duties as a firefighter and an elected member of the District's board of directors are compatible.
This office advises only as to legal issues governed by the Act and does not comment with respect to other laws or
procedures which may regulate the conduct of public officials.

³ The definition of "local government agency" under Section 82041 also would include a special district
which is defined in Section 82048.5 to include, among other things, a county service area and a maintenance district
area.

File No. A-97-386
Page 3

is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official or on a member of the official's immediate
family or on (with respect to the portions relevant in this matter):

"(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

[or]

(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer,
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management."
(Section 87103.)

In order to determine whether you have a conflict of interest that would prohibit your
participation in the board of directors' decisions to consider annexation and property tax
exchange agreements, we must conclude that the decisions will have a foreseeable and material
financial impact on either a source of income to you or on a business entity of which you are,
among other things, a director or an employee. We have chosen not to analyze your situation
under any of the other tests of Section 87103 since they do not appear to apply under the facts set
forth in your letter.⁴ A copy of the text of Section 87103 is enclosed with this letter so you have
a complete version of the statute for your review.

Source of Income

The decisions of the board of directors will have a financial effect on the revenues of both
the District and Yuba City (you state in your letter, "Walton's loss in service area and in dollars
is Yuba City's gain in these instances.").

You are an employee of Yuba City through its fire department and are, presumably, paid
by the city. Therefore, the decisions of the board of directors will impact a source of income to
you, the city. Additionally, while you have not set forth in your letter, you may receive
some kind of compensation from the District as a member of the board of directors; if this is so,
the decisions will also impact your income from the District.

⁴ Your letter mentions possible decisions concerning annexation of certain property into Yuba City's
service area. You do not mention, however, if any real property in which you have an interest will be subject to the
annexation. As you may be aware, a public official may have a conflict of interest if he or she holds an interest in
property which is the subject of the decision before the government agency. Because you have not raised this as an
issue, and give no facts concerning such a scenario, we assume it does not apply to this analysis.

File No. A-97-386
Page 4

The term "income" is specifically defined under the Act in Section 82030 and governs the
application of the word in other sections of the Act, including the conflicts sections. Section
82030 also defines what does not constitute income for purposes of the Act. Subdivision (b)(2)
of this section states that income does not include salary received from a local government
agency.5

We will assume your compensation from both Yuba City and (if applicable) from the
District, would be a salary to you. Since your salary derives from local government agencies,
this compensation would not constitute "income" under the Act. Because you do not meet the
test of Section 87103(c) in that the decisions of the board of directors will not have a foreseeable
or material impact on any source of income to you, you would not have a conflict under this
analysis.

Business Entity of Which You Are a Director or Employee

Given that you are an employee of Yuba City through its fire department and also are one
of the directors of the District's board of directors, this subdivision of Section 87103 appears, on
a facial review, to apply to you. As was quoted above, Section 87103(d) provides that a public
official will have a conflict of interest if a decision in which he or she would participate would
have a foreseeable and material effect on a business entity of which the public official is a
director or employee.

As with the "source of income" analysis, we may dispense with further consideration of
this subdivision as a possible grounds for a conflict of interest since the definition of "business
entity" under the Act does not include either the Yuba City fire department or the District. A
business entity is defined under Section 82005 of the Act as:

"... any organization or enterprise operated for profit, including but not
limited to a proprietorship, partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture,
syndicate, corporation or association." [emphasis added.]

Since neither the fire department nor the District are enterprises "operated for profit," they are
not considered business entities for purposes of the Act. Therefore, the decisions to be engaged
in by the board of directors will not affect business entities of which you are a director or
employee. Under this analysis, you would not have a conflict of interest in participating in the
anticipated decisions described in your letter.

5 We have established previously that the District is a local government agency. Under Section 82041, the
city and its fire department also would be a local government agency.

File No. A-97-386
Page 5

You also asked us to consider possible conflicts for "other city employees who might one
day serve on the Board ...." As I advised Ms. Arline Fisher, the Chairman of the Board, this
office may only advise as to the possible conflicts of persons seeking our advice and may
respond only to those persons or their designated agents. Because the "other city employees"
have not requested our advice, and because it does not appear that you have the authority to seek
this advice on their behalf, we do not comment as to those persons and the information contained
in this letter does not extend to them.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lisa L. Ditora
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:LLD:jlw

Do you have a conflict of interest that would prevent your participation in any vote of the
board of directors of the Walton Fire Protection District concerning issues that affect possible
annexation or revenue agreements with Yuba City?

decisions of the board of directors of the Walton Fire Protection District to consider possible
annexation or revenue agreements with Yuba City.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do you have a conflict of interest that would prevent your participation in any vote of the
board of directors of the Walton Fire Protection District concerning issues that affect possible
annexation or revenue agreements with Yuba City?

CONCLUSION: You have no conflict of interest under the Act and, therefore, may participate in the
decisions of the board of directors of the Walton Fire Protection District to consider possible
annexation or revenue agreements with Yuba City.
